title: Carbon isotope discrimination - part II - gas analysis - v.01

Bioi. 1989. 40:503-37 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"CB10EE338E2246DB8A1BCCB1ED180347","url":"https://link.springer.com/book/10.1007/978-94-011-1566-7"}}}}
steps: Open inlet valve to vacuum (RL)
Switch between Ref and Sample (2x F1) Reference gas

Manually open valve and fill the reference loop with gas Make sure the second valve (top of image) is closed so gas only fills the reference loop. Open the first valve (depicted) for ~5s to fill collection loop.
Close the valve to the reference gas

Open the second valve, to let the reference gas from the connector piece into the inlet Make sure the first valve is closed, then open up the second valve (depicted) to let reference gas out of collection loop into the mass spec. You should see a change in the beam intensity as gas is detected. Wait until this stabilizes so all the gas has left the loop.
Repeat steps 6-9 two times
Check Beam 1 Look at the beam intensity, it should be =< ~ 1e-12. You should expect a value of around 5e-9
V, then B : set target to 6e-9 (Enter) This is to minimize the possibility of adding technical artifacts to measurements by always analysing gas at ~ the same concentration
Do this for both Sample and Ref side

Manually adjust variable volume if the target beam intensity is below or above 6e-9 Take small steps! adjusting the volume +/-5 is sufficient in most cases. The intensity will drfit down over time as gas is consumed by the detector so always good to check both sample and reference immediately before measuring
D (Data processing) + sample name: “Irefref”
Cold finger: N
Run (make sure to feed new paper to the printer!)
Write values down in lab book
Open LVRun actual samples:
Go to Sample side (use F1 F1 to switch from Ref to Sample)
Vacuum Sample side Open "SP" Open "SF" Software view. Diagram of valve system, arrows indicate direction of gas flow
Check Bean 1 intensity => ~ 1e-12 This is to check that the lines are clean, 1e-12 is the value you get when there is little gas left in the system
Return Variable Volume Sample to 0; V A (zero) 0 This is done to reset the bellows for injection of the next sample.
Connect closed sample tube (SI closed)
Vacuum sample inlet Close "SF" Open "SI" (SP already open) Software view. Diagram of valve system, arrows indicate direction of gas flow

Pirani gauge will go to 1e-3, wait 10 more seconds The Pirani gauge gives an indication of the gas pressure in the inlet loop. A value of 1e-3 means the loop is clean of gas so you are ready to inject.
Isolate from pump Close "SP" Close "SI" Open "SF"
Carefully open collection tube (transducer value will rise) Software view. Diagram of valve system, arrow indicates an increase in signal from the Pirani gauge as gas build up behind valve SI

Load sample gas into system Open 'SI' Wait for beam 1 intensity to stabilize. Software view. Diagram of valve system, arrow indicates direction of gas flow. Beam 1 value will rise (if not, then only water in sample; close sample immediately and vacuum!)
Adjust Beam intensity: V B 6e-9
Adjust reference beam intensity Switch to Ref side (F1 F1) V B 6e-9 This is done due to drift in reference beam intensity as gas is used
Esc menu D sample ID Cold finger: N Make sure there is paper in the printer!
Note down values in lab book
Vacuum Sample side Open "SF" Open "SP Return variable volume Sample to 0; V, A, 0 Software view. Diagram of valve system, arrows indicate direction of gas flow
Esc (exit manual control menu)
F (Filer)
D (Data logger)
Select CO2: E (Edit current template)
Use cursor to scroll through to bottom of page for results (don’t change anything!)
Esc to exit
Purge reference linesOpen "RF"Open "RP" Software view. Diagram of valve system, arrows indicate direction of gas flow
Purge reference inletClose RF"Open "RI" Software view. Diagram of valve system, arrows indicate direction of gas flow
Isolate the reference lines and prepare for loading reference gas Close 'RP' Open 'RF' Software view. Diagram of valve system, arrows indicate direction of gas flow
Close the second valve
Flood both sides of the device with reference gas Close "LV" vacuum line Open "RP" Open "SP"Open "SF" Software view. Diagram of valve system, arrows indicate direction of gas flow
Isolate valves Close "RF" Close "RI" Close "SF" Close "SI" Software view. Diagram of valve system, arrows indicate direction of gas flow
Close off inlet Close "SI' Close "SF" Software view. Diagram of valve system, arrows indicate direction of gas flow
Open all valves on both sides except SI and RI This is done to clean out the system ready for the next use. Do not turn off the vacuum pump, it should run continuosly to avoid wearing it out.
creator: Steven J Burgess
